info.title: Deals
info.x-hubspot-api-use-case: When a sales rep starts working with a prospective customer, create a deal record to track the negotiation process. When the customer later makes their purchase, you can update the deal to a closed stage.
info.x-hubspot-introduction: Use the deals API to create and manage CRM records that represent transactions with contacts and/or companies. Deals progress through pipeline stages to reflect whether the transaction has been completed, is active, or has otherwise been lost.

post.summary: Read deals by ID or property

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

post.description: Returns a batch of deals retrieved by internal ID or unique property values, with partial success indicated by a 207 response.

get.description: Read an Object identified by `{dealId}`. `{dealId}` refers to the internal object ID by default, or optionally any unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param.  Control what is returned via the `properties` query param.

parameters.name: dealId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the deal to retrieve.

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned in the response. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: propertiesWithHistory
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned along with their history of previous values. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: associations
parameters.description: A comma separated list of object types to retrieve associated IDs for. If any of the specified associations do not exist, they will be ignored

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

parameters.name: idProperty
parameters.description: The name of a property whose values are unique for this object

delete.description: Move an Object identified by `{dealId}` to the recycling bin.

parameters.name: dealId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the deal to archive.

patch.description: Perform a partial update of an Object identified by `{dealId}`or optionally a unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param. `{dealId}` refers to the internal object ID by default, and the `idProperty` query param refers to a property whose values are unique for the object. Provided property values will be overwritten. Read-only and non-existent properties will result in an error. Properties values can be cleared by passing an empty string.

parameters.name: dealId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the deal to update.

parameters.name: idProperty
parameters.description: The name of a property whose values are unique for this object

post.description: Returns the merged deal object resulting from combining two deals into a single record.

post.summary: Archive a batch of deals by ID
post.description: Archives the specified batch of deals by ID. Returns no content on success.
Returns the batch of newly created deal objects, with partial success indicated by a 207 response.

post.summary: Update deals by ID or property
post.description: Returns the batch of updated deal objects identified by internal ID or unique property values, with partial success indicated by a 207 response.

get.description: Read a page of deals. Control what is returned via the `properties` query param.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to display per page

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: The paging cursor token of the last successfully read resource will be returned as the `paging.next.after` JSON property of a paged response containing more results

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned in the response. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored

parameters.name: propertiesWithHistory
parameters.description: A comma separated list of the properties to be returned along with their history of previous values. If any of the specified properties are not present on the requested object(s), they will be ignored. Usage of this parameter will reduce the maximum number of objects that can be read by a single request

parameters.name: associations
parameters.description: A comma separated list of object types to retrieve associated IDs for. If any of the specified associations do not exist, they will be ignored

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived

post.description: Create a deal with the given properties and return a copy of the object, including the ID. Documentation and examples for creating standard deals is provided.
Create or update records identified by a unique property value as specified by the `idProperty` query param. `idProperty` query param refers to a property whose values are unique for the object.
Returns a collection of deals matching the specified search criteria and filters.
